message.content:
Research tools and libraries that perform tests using prompts/LLM-based assertions. 

Search the web for:
1. "LLM testing framework" 
2. "AI-powered test assertions"
3. "prompt-based testing tools"
4. "LLM evaluation frameworks"

For each tool found, extract:
- Name
- GitHub URL or website
- How it works (brief)
- Key insight/innovation
- Example of usage if available

Focus on tools that allow writing test assertions in natural language that are then evaluated by LLMs.

Return a structured summary of findings.

input.query: LLM testing framework natural language assertions 2025
AI-powered test assertions prompt-based testing tools 2025
natural language test assertions LLM evaluate 2025

Web search results for query: "LLM testing framework natural language assertions 2025"

Links: [{"title":"Introducing LMUnit: Natural language unit testing for LLM evaluation | Contextual AI","url":"https://contextual.ai/research/lmunit"},{"title":"Findings of the Association for Computational Linguistics:","url":"https://aclanthology.org/2025.findings-naacl.449.pdf"},{"title":"Large Language Models for Unit Test Generation: Achievements, Challenges, and the Road Ahead","url":"https://arxiv.org/html/2511.21382v1"},{"title":"GitHub - Shredmetal/llmtest: A behavioral testing library for LLM applications that allows developers to write natural language specifications for unit and integration tests. Validate LLM application behavior using plain English assertions in a simple assert(str, str) form factor.","url":"https://github.com/Shredmetal/llmtest"},{"title":"Test Case Generation for Requirements in Natural Language - An LLM Comparison Study","url":"https://www.researchgate.net/publication/391005625_Test_Case_Generation_for_Requirements_in_Natural_Language_-_An_LLM_Comparison_Study?_tp=eyJjb250ZXh0Ijp7InBhZ2UiOiJzY2llbnRpZmljQ29udHJpYnV0aW9ucyIsInByZXZpb3VzUGFnZSI6bnVsbCwic3ViUGFnZSI6bnVsbH19"},{"title":"LLM Testing Framework: Guide for 2025 - Leanware","url":"https://www.leanware.co/insights/llm-testing-framework-guide"},{"title":"Test Case Generation for Requirements in Natural Language - An LLM Comparison Study (ISEC 2025 - Research Papers) - ISEC 2025","url":"https://conf.researchr.org/details/isec-2025/isec-2025-papers/7/Test-Case-Generation-for-Requirements-in-Natural-Language-An-LLM-Comparison-Study"},{"title":"GitHub - LLM-Testing/LLM4SoftwareTesting","url":"https://github.com/LLM-Testing/LLM4SoftwareTesting"},{"title":"Automated Test Generation Using Large Language Models","url":"https://www.mdpi.com/2306-5729/10/10/156"},{"title":"Testing for LLM Applications: A Practical Guide - Langfuse Blog","url":"https://langfuse.com/blog/2025-10-21-testing-llm-applications"}]

Here are the search results for "LLM testing framework natural language assertions 2025":

## Key Findings

### LMUnit by Contextual AI
The natural language unit test paradigm is simple, cost-effective, and compatible with existing testing frameworks. A unit test is a specific, clear, testable statement or question in natural language about a desirable quality of an LLM's response. Just as traditional unit tests check individual functions in software, unit tests in this paradigm evaluate discrete qualities of individual model outputs – from basic accuracy and formatting to complex reasoning and domain-specific requirements.(no content)LMUnit became open source in July 2025.(no content)As an implementation of this paradigm, LMUnit is a language model optimized for evaluating natural language unit tests, achieving leading averaged performance across preference, direct scoring, and fine-grained unit test evaluation tasks.

### llm_app_test Library
A behavioral testing library for LLM applications that allows developers to write natural language specifications for unit and integration tests, validating LLM application behavior using plain English assertions in a simple assert(str, str) form factor.(no content)String/regex matching and embeddings are too brittle. Embeddings allow slightly more flexibility, but you still need to roughly guess what your LLM-powered app is going to say, and set things like thresholds while having an understanding of vector spaces.(no content)The library was validated with 30,000 test executions with zero format violations and non-determinism only occurring in one case containing a genuine semantic boundary.

### Research on LLM-Based Test Generation
Unit testing is an essential yet laborious technique for verifying software and mitigating regression risks. Although classic automated methods effectively explore program structures, they often lack the semantic information required to produce realistic inputs and assertions. Large Language Models (LLMs) address this limitation by utilizing their data-driven knowledge of code semantics and programming patterns.(no content)A systematic literature review analyzed 115 publications published between May 2021 and August 2025.

### Challenges with LLM Testing
LLMs generate probabilistic outputs influenced by temperature settings, prompt variations, and model updates. A single prompt can yield dozens of valid responses, each phrased differently but semantically equivalent. This probabilistic nature means you can't rely on exact string matching. Instead, you evaluate semantic similarity, factual consistency, and task completion.(no content)Traditional testing relies on predictable outputs. You assert that add(2, 3) returns 5. But when you ask an LLM "What is the capital of France?", you might get "Paris", "The capital is Paris", "Paris, France", or a longer explanation. All are correct, but none match exactly.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Web search results for query: "natural language test assertions LLM evaluate 2025"

Links: [{"title":"Validating Formal Specifications with LLM-generated Test Cases","url":"https://www.arxiv.org/pdf/2510.23350"},{"title":"Automated Test Generation Using Large Language Models","url":"https://www.mdpi.com/2306-5729/10/10/156"},{"title":"AssertionBench: A Benchmark to Evaluate Large- ...","url":"https://aclanthology.org/2025.findings-naacl.449.pdf"},{"title":"GitHub - Shredmetal/llmtest: A behavioral testing library for LLM applications that allows developers to write natural language specifications for unit and integration tests. Validate LLM application behavior using plain English assertions in a simple assert(str, str) form factor.","url":"https://github.com/Shredmetal/llmtest"},{"title":"LLM Evaluation Metrics: The Ultimate LLM Evaluation Guide","url":"https://www.confident-ai.com/blog/llm-evaluation-metrics-everything-you-need-for-llm-evaluation"},{"title":"Do LLMs Generate Useful Test Oracles?","url":"https://www.lucadigrazia.com/papers/ase2025.pdf"},{"title":"Assertion-Aware Test Code Summarization with Large Language Models (AIware 2025 - Main Track) - AIware 2025","url":"https://2025.aiwareconf.org/details/aiware-2025-papers/36/Assertion-Aware-Test-Code-Summarization-with-Large-Language-Models"},{"title":"LLM Testing in 2025: Top Methods and Strategies","url":"https://www.confident-ai.com/blog/llm-testing-in-2024-top-methods-and-strategies"},{"title":"The State Of LLMs 2025: Progress, Progress, and Predictions","url":"https://magazine.sebastianraschka.com/p/state-of-llms-2025"},{"title":"Test Case Generation for Requirements in Natural Language - An LLM Comparison Study | Proceedings of the 18th Innovations in Software Engineering Conference","url":"https://dl.acm.org/doi/10.1145/3717383.3717389"}]

Here are the search results for natural language test assertions and LLM evaluation in 2025:

## Key Findings

### Testing Libraries for Natural Language Assertions

**llmtest** is a behavioral testing library for LLM applications that allows developers to write natural language specifications for unit and integration tests. It lets you validate LLM application behavior using plain English assertions in a simple assert(str, str) form factor.(no content)The library addresses the brittleness of string/regex matching and embeddings - embeddings allow slightly more flexibility, but you still need to roughly guess what your LLM-powered app is going to say, and set things like thresholds while having an understanding of vector spaces.(no content)The library was validated with 30,000 test executions with zero format violations and non-determinism only occurring in one case containing a genuine semantic boundary.

### LLM Evaluation Frameworks

LLM-as-a-judge is described as the most reliable method—using an LLM to evaluate with natural language rubrics, but requires various techniques like G-Eval.(no content)LLM evaluation metrics such as answer correctness, semantic similarity, and hallucination, are metrics that score an LLM system's output based on criteria you care about. They are critical to LLM evaluation, as they help quantify the performance of different LLM systems.

### Research on LLM-Generated Test Cases

A 2025 arXiv paper reports the results of an empirical evaluation of using pre-trained large language models (LLMs) to automate the generation of test cases from natural language requirements, focusing on test cases for structural requirements of simple domain models formalized in the Alloy specification language.(no content)Another study presents a large-scale evaluation to assess the effectiveness of LLMs to generate assertions for test cases added after their training cut-off, highlighting the critical gap in understanding the true generalization ability of LLMs for generating oracles.(no content)Research shows that AI models can help automate test creation for simple code, but their effectiveness decreases for more complex tasks.

### LLM Testing Best Practices

Regression testing involves evaluating an LLM on the same set of test cases every time you make an iteration to safeguard against breaking changes. The upside of using a quantitative LLM evaluation metric is that you can set clear thresholds to define what is considered a "breaking change" and monitor how performance changes through multiple iterations.(no content)Evaluation remains hard, benchmarks are imperfect, and good judgment about when and how to use these systems is still essential.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Web search results for query: "LLM evaluation frameworks prompt testing 2025"

Links: [{"title":"GitHub - confident-ai/deepeval: The LLM Evaluation Framework","url":"https://github.com/confident-ai/deepeval"},{"title":"LLM and Prompt Evaluation Frameworks - Prompting - OpenAI Developer Community","url":"https://community.openai.com/t/llm-and-prompt-evaluation-frameworks/945070"},{"title":"LLM Eval Framework: Guide to Large Language Model Evaluation","url":"https://blog.promptlayer.com/llm-eval-framework/"},{"title":"LLM Testing in 2025: Top Methods and Strategies","url":"https://www.confident-ai.com/blog/llm-testing-in-2024-top-methods-and-strategies"},{"title":"LLM Evaluation: Frameworks, Metrics, and Best Practices | SuperAnnotate","url":"https://www.superannotate.com/blog/llm-evaluation-guide"},{"title":"Top Prompt Evaluation Frameworks in 2025: Helicone, OpenAI Eval, and More","url":"https://www.helicone.ai/blog/prompt-evaluation-frameworks"},{"title":"Building an LLM evaluation framework: best practices | Datadog","url":"https://www.datadoghq.com/blog/llm-evaluation-framework-best-practices/"},{"title":"DeepEval by Confident AI - The LLM Evaluation Framework","url":"https://deepeval.com"},{"title":"5 LLM Evaluation Tools You Should Know in 2025","url":"https://humanloop.com/blog/best-llm-evaluation-tools"},{"title":"LLM Evaluation Frameworks: Head-to-Head Comparison","url":"https://www.comet.com/site/blog/llm-evaluation-frameworks/"}]

Here's what I found about LLM evaluation frameworks for prompt testing in 2025:

## Popular LLM Evaluation Frameworks

### DeepEval
DeepEval is a simple-to-use, open-source LLM evaluation framework for evaluating and testing large-language model systems. It is similar to Pytest but specialized for unit testing LLM outputs. Key features include:
- Support for both end-to-end and component-level LLM evaluation, with a large variety of ready-to-use LLM evaluation metrics powered by any LLM of your choice, statistical methods, or NLP models that run locally.
- Integrates seamlessly with any CI/CD environment.
- Ability to red team your LLM application for 40+ safety vulnerabilities and benchmark LLM apps using datasets to compare which models/prompts work best.

### Promptfoo
Promptfoo is a popular open-source toolkit for prompt testing and evaluation. It allows easy A/B testing of prompts and LLM outputs via simple YAML or CLI configurations, and even supports LLM-as-a-judge evaluations.(no content)It's widely adopted (over 51,000 developers) and requires no complex setup. Promptfoo is especially useful for quick prompt iterations and automated "red-teaming" (e.g., checking for injections or toxic content) in a development workflow.

### Other Notable Frameworks

- **Microsoft Prompt Flow** allows you to create and evaluate complex LLM workflows. It's great for testing multi-step processes and iterating on prompts.

- **LangSmith** offers tools to log and analyze LLM interactions, and includes specialized evaluation capabilities for tasks such as bias detection and safety testing. It's a powerful option if you are building chain-of-thought workflows with LangChain.

- **TruLens** is an open-source library focused on qualitative analysis of LLM responses. TruLens works by injecting feedback functions that run after each LLM call to analyze the result. These feedback functions automatically evaluate the original response—flagging issues like factuality or coherence.

- **RAGAs** is a framework purpose-built for evaluating RAG pipelines. It computes five core metrics – Faithfulness, Contextual Relevancy, Answer Relevancy, Contextual Recall, and Contextual Precision – which together form an overall RAG score.

- **Braintrust** is an end-to-end platform that streamlines LLM development by enabling prompt creation, testing, deployment, and performance tracking in a single workflow.

- **Opik** provides a suite of LLM observability tools to track, evaluate, test, and monitor LLM applications across their development and production lifecycle. It logs complete traces and spans of prompt workflows, supports automated metrics including complex ones like factual correctness via an LLM-as-a-judge.

## Best Practices for LLM Evaluation

Keep a fixed "golden" set of about 200 prompts. This carefully chosen collection, reviewed by experts in the relevant areas, acts as your quality checkpoint. Every new version of your model has to pass this test before it goes live.(no content)Start the evaluation with LLM-as-a-judge. Set up an automated system that can quickly spot outputs that pass or fail your key requirements. This lets you iterate fast and shows you where you need to look more carefully.(no content)For scoring metrics, DeepEval found LLM-Evals (metrics evaluated using LLMs) to perform best and implemented techniques including G-Eval (a SOTA framework for asking LLMs to generate scores based on score rubrics) and DAG (Deep Acyclic Graph) for creating decision-based, LLM as a judge metrics for deterministic scores.

These feedback functions automatically evaluate the original response—flagging issues like factuality or coherence.

Based on my search results, here's a comprehensive overview of AI-powered test assertions and prompt-based testing tools in 2025:

## AI-Powered Test Assertions & Prompt-Based Testing Tools (2025)

### Key Trends
In 2025, AI in test automation has become mainstream.(no content)AI testing tools now use machine learning, NLP, computer vision, and rule-based logic to create, maintain, and optimize tests more efficiently.

### Natural Language/Prompt-Based Assertions

**KaneAI (LambdaTest)**
KaneAI offers AI-Powered Assertions that create logical or visual assertions using natural language, Auto-Healing Locators that locate elements automatically without relying on XPath, and a Low-Code Editor for creating and debugging tests with real-time updates.(no content)It enables expressing complex conditions and assertions in natural language.

**testRigor**
With testRigor, you can use free-flowing plain English to build test automation. testRigor will understand and execute your instructions exactly as written.(no content)testRigor joined the 2025 Inc. 5000 list, ranking among the fastest-growing U.S. companies for its AI-powered software testing innovation.

**Momentic**
Momentic can locate UI elements with just a description, validate assertions with natural language, and provide an interactive browser preview in its intuitive low-code editor.(no content)Users can craft assertions from any logical statement or visual condition using natural language, making it easier to define complex validations without extensive coding.

**Relicx**
Relicx's AI engine automates assertion writing by suggesting relevant assertions after each test step. As users interact with the application and record actions, Relicx intelligently evaluates page states and content, recommending pre-verified assertions for thorough validation.

### Open-Source AI-Driven Frameworks

The landscape of open-source AI-driven UI test automation is dynamic and rapidly evolving. Frameworks like Midscene.js, Magnitude, ZeroStep, Shortest, TestDriver.ai, and Stagehand each offer unique approaches to leveraging AI for enhancing software testing.

**Midscene.js**
A primary strength of Midscene.js is the speed of initial test writing, enabled by its natural language input mechanism. This also contributes to highly readable test scripts. The ability to locate elements by visual characteristics, such as colour, can simplify assertions.

### Prompt Testing for LLMs

AI prompt testing, also known as LLM prompt testing, is a structured process of assessing and validating prompts used with large language models to ensure they generate reliable, accurate, and consistent output. Think of prompt testing as quality assurance for your AI conversations.(no content)There are plenty of popular and powerful prompt testing tools to build and optimize Large Language Model applications available. These tools have features to streamline the prompt engineering workflow around testing, versioning, evaluation, and collaboration.

### Shifting Role of QA Engineers

The role of the QE is likely to shift from being primarily a test coder to becoming more of an AI orchestrator, a sophisticated prompt engineer, a meticulous validator of AI outputs, and a strategic manager of the overall AI-assisted quality process.

With testRigor, you can use free-flowing plain English to build test automation. testRigor will understand and execute your instructions exactly as written.(no content)testRigor joined the 2025 Inc. 5000 list, ranking among the fastest-growing U.S. companies for its AI-powered software testing innovation.

**Momentic**
Momentic can locate UI elements with just a description, validate assertions with natural language, and provide an interactive browser preview in its intuitive low-code editor.(no content)Users can craft assertions from any logical statement or visual condition using natural language, making it easier to define complex validations without extensive coding.

**Relicx**
Relicx's AI engine automates assertion writing by suggesting relevant assertions after each test step. As users interact with the application and record actions, Relicx intelligently evaluates page states and content, recommending pre-verified assertions for thorough validation.

### Open-Source AI-Driven Frameworks

The landscape of open-source AI-driven UI test automation is dynamic and rapidly evolving. Frameworks like Midscene.js, Magnitude, ZeroStep, Shortest, TestDriver.ai, and Stagehand each offer unique approaches to leveraging AI for enhancing software testing.

**Midscene.js**
A primary strength of Midscene.js is the speed of initial test writing, enabled by its natural language input mechanism. This also contributes to highly readable test scripts. The ability to locate elements by visual characteristics, such as colour, can simplify assertions.

### Prompt Testing for LLMs

AI prompt testing, also known as LLM prompt testing, is a structured process of assessing and validating prompts used with large language models to ensure they generate reliable, accurate, and consistent output. Think of prompt testing as quality assurance for your AI conversations.(no content)There are plenty of popular and powerful prompt testing tools to build and optimize Large Language Model applications available. These tools have features to streamline the prompt engineering workflow around testing, versioning, evaluation, and collaboration.

### Shifting Role of QA Engineers

The role of the QE is likely to shift from being primarily a test coder to becoming more of an AI orchestrator, a sophisticated prompt engineer, a meticulous validator of AI outputs, and a strategic manager of the overall AI-assisted quality process.

Here are the search results for "promptfoo LLM testing framework github":

## Promptfoo - LLM Testing Framework

**Promptfoo** is a developer-friendly local tool for testing LLM applications. The main repository is available at [github.com/promptfoo/promptfoo](https://github.com/promptfoo/promptfoo).

### Key Features

Promptfoo allows you to test your prompts, agents, and RAGs. It provides AI red teaming, pentesting, and vulnerability scanning for LLMs. You can compare performance of GPT, Claude, Gemini, Llama, and more, using simple declarative configs with command line and CI/CD integration.

### Getting Started

You can install and run promptfoo with:
```bash
# Install and initialize project
npx promptfoo@latest init

# Run your first evaluation
npx promptfoo eval
```

### Capabilities

Promptfoo enables you to:
- Test your prompts and models with automated evaluations
- Secure your LLM apps with red teaming and vulnerability scanning
- Compare models side-by-side (OpenAI, Anthropic, Azure, Bedrock, Ollama, and more)

### GitHub Action

There's also a dedicated GitHub Action for Promptfoo that lets you test your prompts, agents, and RAGs with AI red teaming, pentesting, and vulnerability scanning for LLMs directly in your CI/CD pipeline. This is available at [github.com/promptfoo/promptfoo-action](https://github.com/promptfoo/promptfoo-action).

The philosophy behind Promptfoo is simple: embrace test-driven development for LLM applications to move beyond the inefficiencies of trial-and-error.

Based on my search results, here's what I found about Auto-Playwright and ZeroStep AI testing with natural language:

## ZeroStep

ZeroStep's AI assistant determines what actions to take at runtime. It allows you to build tests quickly by expressing actions and assertions as plain text instructions.(no content)ZeroStep is an AI-powered testing tool that integrates GPT3.5 and GPT4 technology to make Playwright testing simpler and more adaptable. It uses natural language instructions to script complex interactions and assertions, reducing reliance on selectors and making tests more resilient.

### Key Features:
- ZeroStep's ai() function unlocks the power of GPT3.5 and GPT4 to make Playwright tests simpler and more resilient to change. It interprets natural language inputs and automatically translates them into actionable test code. Testers can describe actions in simple English, such as "Log in" or "Submit the form," without writing complex JavaScript.

- ZeroStep integrates directly with Playwright, allowing users to incorporate AI into their existing Playwright tests without changing their development workflow. Its JavaScript library can be easily integrated into existing test setups.

### Pricing:
The Free Tier offers 500 ai() function calls per month and unlimited users. The Team Tier starts at $20 per month for 2,000 ai() function calls per month and unlimited users.

## Auto-Playwright

Auto-Playwright converts natural-language instructions into executable steps through OpenAI's function-calling interface. This practical use of AI simplifies automation, reduces manual coding, and makes quality assurance more scalable across teams.

### Limitations:
Using Auto Playwright can be challenging in cases where there is not just one element but several elements with the same text. There is a high cost associated with the OpenAI API calls linked to Auto Playwright, which can be prohibitive for some organizations. Occasional failures in API calls can result in inconsistent test results or failing tests.(no content)Based on its limitations and the need for more advanced solutions for demanding test environments, Abstracta does not recommend implementing Auto Playwright as the main test automation tool in a development project.

## Comparison

QA teams who find Codegen's capabilities insufficient may consider AI-based automation testing tools like Auto Playwright or ZeroStep.(no content)You can leverage Jest, Mocha, Jasmine, or other platforms while benefiting from the power of AI with Auto Playwright.

Both tools aim to simplify end-to-end testing by allowing testers to write instructions in natural language rather than dealing with complex selectors and implementation details.

Links: [{"title":"Playwright MCP for AI-Driven Test Automation | Practical Guide","url":"https://www.neovasolutions.com/2025/12/09/playwright-mcp-ai-driven-test-automation/"},{"title":"Modern Test Automation with AI(LLM) and Playwright MCP (Model Context Protocol) | by KailashPathak | Medium","url":"https://kailash-pathak.medium.com/modern-test-automation-with-ai-llm-and-playwright-mcp-model-context-protocol-0c311292c7fb"},{"title":"GitHub - poyro/poyro: Test your web app LLM integrations using existing testing frameworks. Confidently launch AI-driven webapps to production.","url":"https://github.com/poyro/poyro"},{"title":"Modern Test Automation With AI (LLM) and Playwright MCP","url":"https://dzone.com/articles/modern-test-automation-ai-llm-playwright-mcp"},{"title":"Testing prompts with Jest and Vitest | Promptfoo","url":"https://www.promptfoo.dev/docs/integrations/jest/"},{"title":"Testing AI – How to create Automated Prompt Testing With Playwright","url":"https://www.thequalityduck.co.uk/testing-ai-how-to-create-automated-prompt-testing-with-playwright/"},{"title":"How Playwright Test Agents Transform Modern Software Testing","url":"https://www.botgauge.com/blog/playwright-test-agents-ai-qa-automation"},{"title":"GitHub - agorischek/semantic-expect: LLM-based test assertions","url":"https://github.com/agorischek/semantic-expect"},{"title":"GitHub - ryanhaticus/pretzelduck: Use natural language to write end-to-end tests in Playwright.","url":"https://github.com/ryanhaticus/pretzelduck"},{"title":"Using AI LLMs and Playwright MCP to Convert Prompts to Working Test Automation","url":"https://arbisoft.com/blogs/using-ai-llms-and-playwright-mcp-to-convert-prompts-to-working-test-automation"}]

Based on my search, I found several relevant tools and approaches for LLM assertions and natural language testing with Vitest and Playwright:

## Key Tools & Libraries

### 1. **Poyro** (GitHub: poyro/poyro)
Poyro lets you test your web app LLM integrations using existing testing frameworks, helping you confidently launch AI-driven webapps to production. It provides a `toFulfillCriterion` matcher that can determine whether an LLM output meets or does not meet a natural language criterion.(no content)The first time you run a test, the model file for the locally run LLM will be downloaded, which should take a couple of minutes.

### 2. **Semantic Expect** (GitHub: agorischek/semantic-expect)
This library provides LLM-based test assertions for Vitest and Jest with syntax like `await expect(writeJoke).toGenerate('Something funny')`. It addresses the challenge that developing LLM-backed applications requires redefining "reliability" since LLMs are fundamentally non-deterministic.(no content)Semantic Expect's role is to shift basic validation left and verify essential generative behavior before shipping.

### 3. **Promptfoo** (Jest/Vitest Integration)
Promptfoo can be integrated with test frameworks like Jest and Vitest to evaluate prompts as part of existing testing and CI workflows, with examples showing how to create test cases using semantic similarity and LLM grading. It provides matchers like `toMatchSemanticSimilarity` and `toPassLLMRubric`.

### 4. **PretzelDuck** (GitHub: ryanhaticus/pretzelduck)
PretzelDuck lets you use natural language to write end-to-end tests in Playwright with syntax like `pretzelDuck.test("find more information about protocol registries", "you should be on a page titled Protocol Registries")`.

### 5. **Playwright MCP (Model Context Protocol)**
Playwright MCP is a server that acts as a bridge between Large Language Models (LLMs) or other agents and Playwright-managed browsers. It enables structured command execution, allowing AI to control web interactions like navigation, form filling, or assertions.

## Testing Approach Recommendations
The current best approach for LLM testing is to use a combination of traditional assertions and an Evaluator LLM. This way you can use traditional assertions to check the response contains (or does not contain) specific key words and use the Evaluator LLM to provide confidence over the semantic similarity to an expected answer.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Links: [{"title":"Overview: Tracing - Phoenix","url":"https://arize.com/docs/phoenix/tracing/llm-traces"},{"title":"Home - Phoenix - Arize AI","url":"https://phoenix.arize.com/"},{"title":"GitHub - Arize-ai/phoenix: AI Observability & Evaluation","url":"https://github.com/Arize-ai/phoenix"},{"title":"Getting Started With LLM Evaluation Using Phoenix - Arize AI","url":"https://arize.com/resource/getting-started-with-llm-evaluation-using-phoenix/"},{"title":"LLM Tracing and Observability - Arize Phoenix","url":"https://phoenix.arize.com/llm-tracing-and-observability-with-arize-phoenix/"},{"title":"Evaluator Traces | Arize Phoenix","url":"https://arize.com/docs/phoenix/evaluation/llm-evals/evaluator-traces"},{"title":"Log Evaluation Results | Phoenix","url":"https://docs.arize.com/phoenix/tracing/how-to-tracing/feedback-and-annotations/llm-evaluations"},{"title":"Running Evals on Traces | Arize Phoenix","url":"https://arize.com/docs/phoenix/tracing/how-to-tracing/feedback-and-annotations/evaluating-phoenix-traces"},{"title":"Overview: Evals | Arize Phoenix","url":"https://arize.com/docs/phoenix/evaluation/llm-evals"},{"title":"phoenix/tutorials/evals/evals_quickstart.ipynb at main · Arize-ai/phoenix","url":"https://github.com/Arize-ai/phoenix/blob/main/tutorials/evals/evals_quickstart.ipynb"}]

Here are the search results for "Arize Phoenix LLM evaluation tracing":

## Arize Phoenix: LLM Evaluation & Tracing Platform

Arize Phoenix is an open-source LLM tracing & evaluation platform.(no content)It allows you to seamlessly instrument, experiment, and optimize AI applications in real time—transparent, framework-agnostic, and vendor-lock-in free.

### Tracing Capabilities

LLM tracing records the paths taken by requests as they propagate through multiple steps or components of an LLM application.(no content)For example, when a user interacts with an LLM application, tracing can capture the sequence of operations, such as document retrieval, embedding generation, language model invocation, and response generation to provide a detailed timeline of the request's execution.(no content)Phoenix traces AI applications via OpenTelemetry and has first-class integrations with LlamaIndex, LangChain, OpenAI, and others.

### Key Features

Phoenix's tracing helps identify:
- **Application Latency**: Slow invocations of LLMs, Retrievers, and other components
- **Token Usage**: Detailed breakdown of token usage for LLM calls
- **Runtime Exceptions**: Critical runtime exceptions like rate-limiting events
- **Retrieved Documents**: Inspection of documents retrieved during Retriever calls

### LLM Evaluation

The Arize Phoenix LLM Evals open source library is designed for simple, fast, and accurate LLM-based evaluations. It leverages a variety of LLM evaluation metrics and tracing.(no content)Phoenix Evals automatically traces all evaluation executions, providing complete transparency into how your evaluators make decisions. This visibility is essential for achieving human alignment and building trust in your evaluation results.

### Framework Support

Phoenix is vendor and language agnostic with out-of-the-box support for popular frameworks (LlamaIndex, LangChain, Haystack, DSPy, smolagents) and LLM providers (OpenAI, Bedrock, MistralAI, VertexAI, LiteLLM, Google GenAI and more).

### Deployment Options

Phoenix runs practically anywhere, including your local machine, a Jupyter notebook, a containerized deployment, or in the cloud. Phoenix container images are available via Docker Hub and can be deployed using Docker or Kubernetes. Arize AI also provides cloud instances at app.phoenix.arize.com.

Web search results for query: "Langfuse evaluation testing LLM assertions"

Links: [{"title":"LLM-as-a-Judge Evaluation - Langfuse","url":"https://langfuse.com/docs/evaluation/evaluation-methods/llm-as-a-judge"},{"title":"Evaluation of LLM Applications - Langfuse","url":"https://langfuse.com/docs/evaluation/overview"},{"title":"Testing for LLM Applications: A Practical Guide - Langfuse Blog","url":"https://langfuse.com/blog/2025-10-21-testing-llm-applications"},{"title":"Evaluate Langfuse LLM Traces with an External Evaluation Pipeline - Langfuse","url":"https://langfuse.com/guides/cookbook/example_external_evaluation_pipelines"},{"title":"GitHub - langfuse/langfuse: 🪢 Open source LLM engineering platform: LLM Observability, metrics, evals, prompt management, playground, datasets. Integrates with OpenTelemetry, Langchain, OpenAI SDK, LiteLLM, and more. 🍊YC W23","url":"https://github.com/langfuse/langfuse"},{"title":"LLM Evaluation 101: Best Practices, Challenges & Proven Techniques - Langfuse Blog","url":"https://langfuse.com/blog/2025-03-04-llm-evaluation-101-best-practices-and-challenges"},{"title":"Evaluate Langfuse LLM Traces with UpTrain - Langfuse","url":"https://langfuse.com/guides/cookbook/evaluation_with_uptrain"},{"title":"Automated Evaluations of LLM Applications - Langfuse Blog","url":"https://langfuse.com/blog/2025-09-05-automated-evaluations"},{"title":"Evaluating LLM Applications: A Comprehensive Roadmap - Langfuse Blog","url":"https://langfuse.com/blog/2025-11-12-evals"},{"title":"Langfuse Documentation - Langfuse","url":"https://langfuse.com/docs"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Langfuse evaluation testing for LLM assertions:

## Langfuse LLM Evaluation Overview

Langfuse is an open source LLM engineering platform that helps teams collaboratively develop, monitor, evaluate, and debug AI applications.

### Evaluation Methods

With Langfuse you can capture all your LLM evaluations in one place. You can combine a variety of different evaluation metrics like model-based evaluations (LLM-as-a-Judge), human annotations or fully custom evaluation workflows via API/SDKs.

### LLM-as-a-Judge Evaluation

LLM-as-a-Judge (also known as Model-based Evaluations) is an evaluation method to score the output of an application by using an LLM as an evaluator. The LLM is given a trace or a dataset entry and asked to score and reason about the output. The resulting scores include chain-of-thought reasoning as a comment.

Key benefits include:
- Scalable: Judge thousands of outputs quickly versus human annotators.
- Repeatable: With a fixed rubric, you can rerun the same prompts to get consistent scores.

### Testing LLM Applications

This variability doesn't mean we can't test LLM applications—it means we need different testing strategies. In LLM applications, these concepts blend together. You "test" your application by "evaluating" its outputs with scoring functions. A test passes if the evaluation score meets your threshold.(no content)Think of these as automated regression tests that verify your LLM application maintains acceptable quality as you make changes. The "unit" being tested is your application's behavior on specific inputs.

### Assertion-Based Testing Example

Langfuse provides an Experiment Runner SDK for testing with assertions. You could also use more sophisticated evaluators, including LLM-as-a-judge for semantic similarity. The accuracy evaluator checks if the expected answer is in the output, returning an Evaluation with a value of 1.0 or 0.0 based on whether the expected output appears in the response.

### External Evaluation Pipelines

This cookbook explains how to build an external evaluation pipeline to measure the performance of your production LLM application using Langfuse. As a rule of thumb, we encourage you to check first if the evaluations in the Langfuse UI cover your use case. If your needs go beyond these, you can still implement in Langfuse custom evaluation templates without code.(no content)Langfuse can handle numerical, boolean and categorical (string) scores. Wrapping your custom evaluation logic in a function is often a good practice. Evaluation functions should take a trace as input and yield a valid score.

### Automated Evaluation Best Practices

Manually annotating outputs after every modification is slow and expensive, especially when you want to integrate evaluations into a CI/CD pipeline. Automated evaluators solve this problem by providing a scalable way to measure and monitor your application's failure modes, enabling a fast and effective development loop.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.
